hypothesis: If a GraphSAGE model that ingests frozen latent features from pre-trained single-gas FNNs along with gas fractions is used to predict Ar/O2 mixture radial etching rates, then it will achieve lower weighted MSE (higher R²) than direct FNN, GCN, or GAT baselines—including under withheld high power/pressure regions and when trained only on xAr>0.2—because gas-specific latent representations enable better generalization to unseen mixture compositions and operating regimes.
